title: hieracium jurassicum subsp. tephroprenanthes
intro: Hieracium jurassicum subsp. tephroprenanthes, also known as Jurassic Hawkweed, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. As a member of the Compositae family, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Jurassic Hawkweed can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments, although proper identification and dosing are essential due to the plant's potential toxicity and limited availability.
